subject: Human Resources
title: Paid Time Off Policies
description: A short lesson to help you apply Paid Time Off Policies.

hook.text: A company offers 'unlimited PTO.' Employees actually take less time off—because there's no clear number to use.

buildup.text: PTO policies cover vacation, sick leave, personal days, and holidays—critical for rest and preventing burnout.

discovery.text: The U.S. has no federal mandate for paid vacation. Most developed nations require 20-30 days minimum.

twist.text: Unlimited PTO sounds generous but often backfires—without norms, employees take less, not more, fearing judgment.

climax.text: The best policies set minimum PTO requirements—ensuring people actually rest—rather than removing structure entirely.

punchline.text: Time off works when people feel safe enough to take it.

quiz.question: Why does unlimited PTO often backfire?
options: Employees take less time off due to unclear norms and fear of judgment
Employees take too much time off
